name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-30
title: Full rabbit-cage spec.md monotonic invariant renumber + cross-reference audit (split from BACKLOG-29 scope-limit)
description:
Discovered when PR #163 (CONTRACT-BACKLOG-30) landed the new check_invariant_monotonic_order enforcement test, with rabbit-feature already pruned from KNOWN_ISSUES but rabbit-cage still required.

Background:
- RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-29 was scope-limited (per ws35 wave-2 orchestration) to relocating only Inv 86/87/88/89 to monotonic position.
- rabbit-cage spec.md has 7 'Invariants' sections (3 ##, 4 ###). Several still contain non-monotonic numbering:
    * '## Invariants (additional)': 20 -> 12 (one violation)
    * '## Invariants (additional)': 52 -> 13 (another)
    * '## Invariants (additional)': 42 -> 15 (another)
    * '### Invariants': 85 -> 36 (final)
- These violations are pre-existing; cycle 3 didn't target them. The new enforcement test catches them on first run.

Scope (mirrors CONTRACT-BACKLOG-31 pattern):
- Pre-requisite: grep audit — generate a manifest of every cross-reference to rabbit-cage invariants in .claude/ (tests, SKILL.md, other features' spec.md citing 'rabbit-cage Inv N'). The manifest IS the work plan.
- Renumber all 7 Invariants sections monotonically. Decide whether each section should restart at 1 or continue from prior section's max (the current pattern is the latter; preserve unless there's a stronger structural reason).
- Update every cross-reference identified by the manifest.
- Remove rabbit-cage from _MONOTONIC_KNOWN_ISSUES in .claude/features/contract/lib/checks.py once clean.

Acceptance:
- All 7 rabbit-cage Invariants sections monotonic.
- check_invariant_monotonic_order passes for rabbit-cage with rabbit-cage NOT on KNOWN_ISSUES.
- All cross-reference sites updated.
- No tests broken.

Companion to CONTRACT-BACKLOG-31 (contract's own renumber). When both land, KNOWN_ISSUES becomes empty.

history.note: Closed by PR #166: monotonic renumber 1..90 across 7 sections + cross-reference audit; _MONOTONIC_KNOWN_ISSUES now = []
